title: Reynolds' Lead Citrate Stain
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"at2g2","text":"Reynolds Lead Citrate Stain for electron microscopy is a standard stain developed by Edward S. Reynolds and consists of a mixture of 80 mM Lead(II) nitrate and 120 mM Sodium citrate. Sodium hydroxide is added to make the stain basic to pH 12.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"64unk","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"53ngr","text":"Preparation time:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":17}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"anem","text":"40-60 min","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b7ab9","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bfa7p","text":"Reference:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":10}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5hutl","text":"Edward S. Reynolds; THE USE OF LEAD CITRATE AT HIGH pH AS AN ELECTRON-OPAQUE STAIN IN ELECTRON MICROSCOPY .J Cell Biol1 April 1963; 17 (1): 208–212. doi:https://doi.org/10.1083/jcb.17.1.208","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":107,"length":11}],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":153,"length":36}],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"76D7FDDD971711EDB7970A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://doi.org/10.1083/jcb.17.1.208"}}}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"dc6p6","text":"This protocols aim to show the exact method that the Centre for Cellular Imaging at the University of Gothenburg use to prepare a standard buffer.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2o10h","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3m2jv","text":"We do not claim to have discovered, invented or otherwise hold any form of intellectual property of the buffer(s) described in this protocol.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bs3bv","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"391qd","text":"Feel free to visit us: https://www.gu.se/en/core-facilities/centre-for-cellular-imaging","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":23,"length":64}],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"475FAFA6972511EDB7970A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://www.gu.se/en/core-facilities/centre-for-cellular-imaging"}}}}

May damage the unborn child.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1uqqs","text":"H372 Causes damage to organs (Blood, Central nervous system, Immune system, Kidney) through prolonged or repeated exposure.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5nftu","text":"H410 Very toxic to aquatic life with long lasting effects.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a6kc8","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bf7ka","text":"  ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"ca3s8","text":"Not a hazardous substance or mixture according to Regulation (EC) No 1272/2008.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cs4to","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9l6o","text":"  ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":2,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"2q06u","text":"H290 May be corrosive to metals. steps: Weigh out and dissolve Lead (II) nitrate and tri-Sodium citrate dihydrate: Weigh out 1.32 g of Lead(II) nitrate (EMSURE®) Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #1073980100 (CAS: 10099-74-8) in a 50 ml volumetric flask in which you have added a small magnetic stirrer. Dissolve the Lead(II) nitrate by adding 30 mL of Milli-Q water Contributed by users and turning on the stirrer. Weigh out 1.76 g of Tri-Sodium citrate dihydrate (EMSURE®) Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #1064480500 (CAS: 6132-04-3) in a small weighing boat. Add it to the dissolved Lead(II) nitrate. Shake the mixture vigorously, or vortex it for 00:01:00 The solution will be milky. Cover the volumetric flask with parafilm and let it stir for an additional 00:30:00 Prepare a 1 M sodium hydroxide (NaOH) solution: Add 10 mL of Milli-Q water Contributed by users to a 15 ml falcon tube. Weigh out 0.4 g of Sodium hydroxide BioXtra ≥98% (acidimetric) pellets (anhydrous) Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #S8045-1KG (CAS: 1310-73-2) on a small weighing paper, and add the NaOH to the water. Mix the NaOH until it dissolves by inverting the falcon tube. If your lab have a stock solution of NaOH, this can be diluted to 1 M and used instead of preparing it fresh as described above. Finalizing the Lead citrate solution: Add 8 mL of the 1 M NaOH solution to the Lead(II) nitrate/Tri-Sodium citrate mixture, this will raise the final pH to 12±0.1. Keep stirring until the solution is clear. Add Milli-Q water Contributed by users until the volume is 50 ml. Aliquote the mixture in: 5 x 10 mL - 10 ml syringes labelled: Lead citrate and date. Storage: Mix on the day of use for optimal staining. For longer storage, seal syringes with parafilm and store long term (4 weeks) at 4 °C If using stored lead citrate, carfully inspect before usage for precipitates. Do not use if precipitates are formed. Always ad a 0.2 µm filter to the syringe before use. Clean up: Rinse the 50 ml volumetric flask and magnetic stirrer twice with water, pouring the wash of in a liquid lead waste. Then rinse thouroghly with water before before putting them in the dishwasher.
creator: Jens Berndtsson
